page: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 7.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local SSD storage.

Benchmark results indicate that this server sits in the lower performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as weak, falling in the bottom 25% with a score of 1072.92, while multi-core performance is poor, falling in the bottom 10% with a score of 2156.87. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 25.07 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 19.33 GB/sec. Geekbench scores also fall into the bottom 10%, with a single-core score of 805.0 and a multi-core score of 1426.0.

Given the hardware profile and benchmark outcomes, the b2-7 is suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 2.84 GB/sec, or simple Redis key-value operations, processing 3.11 million SET operations per second. It is not designed for resource-intensive tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or complex database operations, which show poor to weak performance in testing. The server represents a low-cost entry point for development, testing, or minor utility services requiring dedicated CPU resources without high computational demands.
description: The OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Core Processor (Haswell) running at 2.0 GHz. It offers 2 dedicated vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data places this instance in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng single-core performance in the bottom 25% and multi-core performance in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large block writes averaging 19.33 GB/sec. Consequently, this server is best suited for low-demand applications, such as basic static web serving (achieving 2.84 GB/sec throughput) or lightweight Redis operations, rather than intensive computational tasks like compilation or complex database processing. It represents a resource-dense, entry-level option for non-critical development environments.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server configured with 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 7 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage, suitable for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 7 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with local SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web hosting.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated x86_64 Intel vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
7.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local SSD storage
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization with 1 public IPv4 address
Suitable for lightweight static web serving and basic Redis operations
Lower-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance profiles
